ground_truth.title: Streaming services
ground_truth.main_content: Latest news, analysis and opinion on Streaming services. This Financial Times topic page aggregates coverage of the streaming industry including the major news that Netflix has agreed to acquire Warner Bros Discovery in a landmark $83bn deal. Coverage examines the key players in the battle for Warner Bros including Saudi Arabia's crown prince and California tech moguls. Reports detail how Hollywood is shocked by the looming sale and how the deal stands to undercut earnings for virtually every other segment of the industry. Analysis explores Netflix's antitrust implications and how the streaming pioneer has upended the industry over two decades. Coverage also includes reports on Paramount's competing offer with assistance from Jared Kushner and Middle East funds, job concerns from actors and screenwriters, and Disney's performance as its film and TV units slide. Additional stories cover Spotify's planned US price increases and David Ellison's overtures for Warner Bros.
with: Netflix has agreed to acquire Warner Bros Discovery in a landmark $83bn deal.
Who matters in the battle for Warner Bros? From Saudi Arabia's crown prince to California tech moguls, key players in the fight for a celebrated Hollywood institution.
Hollywood is shocked: Warner Bros sale looms large over movie industry, with deal set to undercut earnings for virtually every other segment.
Paramount gatecrashes Warner Bros-Netflix deal with Jared Kushner and Middle East funds assisting with all-cash offer.
The Netflix age has been great for consumers but terrible for artists as returns to musicians and writers are dwindling fast.
without: Search for stories, topics or securities
